MCP Semantic Query (db.semantic.query)

This page documents the semantic-query engine under:

com.github.salilvnair.convengine.engine.mcp.query.semantic

It is the Text-to-AST-to-SQL runtime for ConvEngine MCP mode semantic.

Core contract

Semantic Query generates AST JSON with LLM, compiles SQL deterministically in Java, enforces policy guardrails, executes read-only SQL, and returns auditable result summaries.

Runtime request flow​

Semantic Query Runtime

MCP planner -> semantic stages -> planner answer

Execution Steps
Press enter or space to select a node.You can then use the arrow keys to move the node around. Press delete to remove it and escape to cancel.
Press enter or space to select an edge. You can then press delete to remove it or escape to cancel.
Step Detail

4. SemanticQueryRuntimeService

SEMANTIC_RUNTIME

Orchestrates deterministic stage pipeline and stage error handling.

Session Snapshot
  • stage outputs + _meta
Tables Touched
  • ce_audit (W)
  • ce_verbose (R optional)

Stage pipeline (actual order)​

Semantic stage pipeline

OrderStagePrimary classDeterminants to watch
1RetrievalSemanticRetrievalStageRETRIEVAL_DONE / RETRIEVAL_ERROR
2Join pathSemanticJoinPathStageJOIN_PATH_RESOLVED / JOIN_PATH_ERROR
3AST generationSemanticAstGenerationStageAST_INPUT, AST_OUTPUT, AST_GENERATED / AST_ERROR, AST_GENERATION_ERROR
4AST validationSemanticAstValidationStageAST_VALIDATED / AST_VALIDATION_ERROR
5SQL compileSemanticSqlCompileStageSQL_COMPILED / SQL_COMPILE_ERROR
6SQL executeSemanticSqlExecuteStageSQL_EXECUTED / SQL_EXECUTE_ERROR
7Result summarySemanticResultSummaryStageRESULT_SUMMARIZED / SUMMARY_ERROR
-Runtime wrapperSemanticQueryRuntimeServiceRUNTIME_ERROR

Package Responsibilities​

Package responsibility map

PackageWhat it holdsExamples
ast/*AST contracts + normalize + validate + version->canonical mappingSemanticQueryAstV1, AstFilterGroup, DefaultAstValidator, V1CanonicalAstMapper
retrieval/*Entity/table retrieval models + retrievers + vector adaptersCandidateEntity, RetrievalResult, DefaultSemanticEntityTableRetriever, PostgresPgVectorSearchAdapter
graph/*Schema graph + join path planning contracts/providersSchemaGraphEngine, JoinPathResolver, JGraphTSchemaGraphEngine, DefaultJoinPathResolver
llm/*LLM AST JSON generationDefaultSemanticAstGenerator
sql/*AST->SQL compile core + clause/operator/window handlers + policyDefaultSemanticSqlCompiler, DefaultAstQueryAssemblyClauseHandler, DefaultInAstOperatorHandler
execute/*Compiled SQL executionDefaultSemanticSqlExecutor
summary/*Result formattingDefaultSemanticResultSummarizer
runtime/*MCP tool entry + pipeline + stage orchestrationDbSemanticQueryToolHandler, SemanticQueryRuntimeService, SemanticStagePipelineFactory
model/*Semantic model loading and registrySemanticModelLoader, SemanticModelRegistry

Libraries we use​

Semantic Query runtime is built with a small set of focused libraries in convengine/pom.xml.

Core dependencies (actual)​

<dependency>
  <groupId>org.jooq</groupId>
  <artifactId>jooq</artifactId>
</dependency>

<dependency>
  <groupId>org.jgrapht</groupId>
  <artifactId>jgrapht-core</artifactId>
  <version>1.5.2</version>
</dependency>

<dependency>
  <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.dataformat</groupId>
  <artifactId>jackson-dataformat-yaml</artifactId>
</dependency>

What each library is doing in semantic mode

LibraryWhere usedRole in runtime
jOOQsql/handler/* + sql/compiler/*Builds SQL AST fragments and named-parameter SQL deterministically from canonical semantic AST.
JGraphTgraph/provider/JGraphTSchemaGraphEngineSchema graph traversal + shortest join path resolution from FK metadata.
Jackson YAMLmodel/SemanticModelLoaderLoads semantic model (`semantic-layer.yaml`) into runtime model objects.

jOOQ in Semantic Query​

jOOQ is not used as a codegen-first ORM here. It is used as a deterministic SQL construction layer controlled by the semantic compiler pipeline.

Flow:

  1. LLM generates AST JSON.
  2. AST is canonicalized and validated.
  3. SQL handlers build clauses with jOOQ DSL + controlled SQL tokens.
  4. SQL is emitted with named params.
  5. JDBC executes with bind values.

jOOQ conversion examples​

  • Example 1: EQ + ILIKE
  • Example 2: IN + BETWEEN + NULL checks
  • Example 3: ROW_NUMBER window
{
  "entity": "DisconnectRequest",
  "where": {
    "op": "AND",
    "conditions": [
      {"field": "requestStatus", "op": "EQ", "value": "FAILED"},
      {"field": "teamNotes", "op": "ILIKE", "value": "%inventory%"}
    ],
    "groups": []
  }
}
// DefaultComparisonAstOperatorHandler
return DSL.condition("{0} " + sqlToken(operator) + " {1}", field, context.nextParam(value));
... WHERE ("t1"."request_status" = :p1 AND "t2"."zp_asr_team_notes" ILIKE :p2)
{
  "entity": "DisconnectRequest",
  "where": {
    "op": "AND",
    "conditions": [
      {"field": "accountId", "op": "IN", "value": ["UPSA100", "ACMA200"]},
      {"field": "requestedAt", "op": "BETWEEN", "value": ["2026-03-01T00:00:00Z", "2026-03-05T00:00:00Z"]},
      {"field": "disconnectId", "op": "IS_NULL", "value": null}
    ],
    "groups": []
  }
}
// DefaultInAstOperatorHandler + DefaultBetweenAstOperatorHandler + DefaultNullCheckAstOperatorHandler
field.in(params)
DSL.condition("{0} BETWEEN {1} AND {2}", field, context.nextParam(v1), context.nextParam(v2))
field.isNull()
... WHERE ("t1"."account_id" IN (:p1, :p2)
       AND "t1"."requested_at" BETWEEN :p3 AND :p4
       AND "t0"."zp_disconnect_id" IS NULL)
{
  "entity": "DisconnectRequest",
  "windows": [
    {
      "name": "account_rank",
      "function": "ROW_NUMBER",
      "partition_by": ["accountId"],
      "order_by": [{"field": "requestedAt", "direction": "DESC", "nulls": "LAST"}]
    }
  ]
}
// DefaultRowNumberWindowFunctionHandler
return "ROW_NUMBER() OVER (" + overClause + ")";
ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY "t1"."account_id" ORDER BY "t1"."requested_at" DESC NULLS LAST) AS "account_rank"

JGraphT in Semantic Query​

JGraphT powers schema traversal and shortest join-path resolution. It prevents LLM from inventing joins by computing paths from real FK metadata.

Flow:

  1. Graph engine refreshes vertices/edges from schema inspector FK joins.
  2. Join-path resolver chooses base table and initial required tables from retrieval.
  3. SQL compile expands required tables from AST references (projections, where, having, exists, subquery, sort, group_by, time_range, metrics) before final join assembly.
  4. Shortest paths are found with hop limits.
  5. Resolved edges are de-duplicated and returned to compiler.

JGraphT traversal examples​

  • Example 1: Graph build
  • Example 2: Shortest path
  • Example 3: Resolve plan
// JGraphTSchemaGraphEngine.refreshGraph()
for (Map<String, Object> row : joins) {
  String sourceTable = text(row.get("source_table"));
  String targetTable = text(row.get("target_table"));
  nextGraph.addVertex(sourceTable);
  nextGraph.addVertex(targetTable);
  nextGraph.addEdge(sourceTable, targetTable);
  nextGraph.addEdge(targetTable, sourceTable); // reverse edge
}
Result: directed graph with forward + reverse FK edges for traversal flexibility.
// JGraphTSchemaGraphEngine.shortestPath()
while (!queue.isEmpty()) {
  NodePath current = queue.poll();
  if (current.node().equals(toTable)) {
    return toEdges(current.path());
  }
  for (String next : neighbors(current.node())) {
    if (!visited.contains(next)) {
      queue.add(new NodePath(next, nextPath));
      visited.add(next);
    }
  }
}
Uses BFS-style traversal; returns concrete SchemaEdge list for SQL joins.
// DefaultJoinPathResolver.doResolve()
String base = candidates.get(0).name();
for (String target : requiredTables) {
  List<SchemaEdge> path = graph.shortestPath(base, target, maxHops);
  if (path == null || path.isEmpty()) unresolved.add(target);
  else merge(path);
}
return new JoinPathPlan(base, merged, requiredTables, unresolved, confidence);
Compiler receives resolved edges and unresolved table list; validator blocks unresolved joins.

AST model capabilities​

Operators in AST model​

AstOperator.java includes:

  • ILIKE
  • BETWEEN
  • IN
  • NOT_IN
  • IS_NULL
  • IS_NOT_NULL

Normalization behavior in AstFilter/AstSubqueryFilter also accepts aggregate-style tokens and maps to base operators:

  • COUNT_GT -> GT
  • SUM_GTE -> GTE
  • COUNT_NOT_IN -> NOT_IN
  • *_NOTNULL -> IS_NOT_NULL

New AST nodes​

  • Boolean tree: AstFilterGroup.java
  • Exists: AstExistsBlock.java
  • Subquery filters: AstSubqueryFilter.java, AstSubquerySpec.java
  • Windows: AstWindowSpec.java
  • Full v1 payload: SemanticQueryAstV1.java

Correlated field reference rule​

For exists.where and subquery_filters.where, a value starting with $ is treated as an outer-field reference, not a string literal.

  • $disconnectId means "compare against outer query field disconnectId"
  • Resolver maps that field using outer entity semantic field mapping + outer aliases

Example AST:

{
  "exists": [
    {
      "entity": "DisconnectRequest",
      "where": {
        "op": "AND",
        "conditions": [
          { "field": "disconnectId", "op": "=", "value": "$disconnectId" }
        ],
        "groups": []
      },
      "not_exists": false
    }
  ]
}

Compiled SQL shape:

EXISTS (
  SELECT 1
  FROM zp_request ex1
  LEFT JOIN zp_disconnect_order exj2 ON ex1.zp_request_id = exj2.zp_request_id
  WHERE exj2.zp_disconnect_id = t0.zp_disconnect_id
)

Validation coverage​

DefaultAstValidator.java validates:

  • entity and field existence
  • operator and value compatibility (ILIKE string-only, BETWEEN two values, IN/NOT_IN array)
  • exists blocks
  • subquery filters
  • window specs
  • groupBy/metric constraints

SQL rendering coverage​

  • LIKE/comparison: DefaultComparisonAstOperatorHandler.java
  • BETWEEN: DefaultBetweenAstOperatorHandler.java
  • IN/NOT_IN: DefaultInAstOperatorHandler.java
  • IS_NULL/IS_NOT_NULL: DefaultNullCheckAstOperatorHandler.java
  • ROW_NUMBER window: DefaultRowNumberWindowFunctionHandler.java

DATE_TRUNC status​

Not active yet:

  • Placeholder exists in DefaultFunctionClauseHandler.java
  • Missing concrete AST field + validator + function handler for DATE_TRUNC

Static config and runtime config requirements​

Static DB rows required​

Static table checklist for semantic flow

TableRequiredPurpose
ce_configYesDefaultSemanticAstGenerator prompts/schema (SYSTEM_PROMPT, USER_PROMPT, SCHEMA_PROMPT).
ce_intentYesSEMANTIC_QUERY intent registration.
ce_intent_classifierYesRoute semantic questions to SEMANTIC_QUERY.
ce_prompt_templateYesSCHEMA_JSON and DERIVED templates for SEMANTIC_QUERY.
ce_responseYesCOMPLETED/FAILED response mapping.
ce_ruleYesANALYZE bootstrap + MCP outcome transitions + reset behavior.
ce_mcp_toolYesRegister db.semantic.query in MCP scope.
ce_mcp_plannerYesPlanner prompt for SEMANTIC_QUERY/ANALYZE.
ce_verboseYesStage + AST LLM + runtime determinant messaging.
ce_output_schemaOptionalExtraction helper schema for semantic prompts.

Application YAML required​

mcp:
  db:
    query:
      mode: semantic # semantic-catalog | knowledge-graph | semantic

    semantic-catalog:
      enabled: true

    knowledge-graph:
      enabled: true

    semantic:
      enabled: true
      model-path: classpath:/mcp/semantic-layer.yaml

Request flow by package (quick reference)​

  1. runtime/handler: DbSemanticQueryToolHandler receives db.semantic.query.
  2. runtime/core + runtime/pipeline + runtime/stage/*: SemanticQueryRuntimeService runs stage DAG.
  3. retrieval/*: ranks candidate entity/tables.
  4. graph/*: resolves join edges/path.
  5. llm/*: generates AST JSON.
  6. ast/*: canonicalize + normalize + validate.
  7. sql/*: deterministic compile + policy.
  8. execute/*: SQL run + row map.
  9. summary/*: final user-facing summary payload.
  10. model/*: shared semantic-layer model registry.

Semantic Query runtime path​

Full runtime lifecycle

This is the complete end-to-end Semantic Query path for one turn. It follows the same Database Knowledge Graph runtime style, but each stage is specific to semantic retrieval, AST generation, deterministic SQL compile, execution, and response shaping.

1User request

Responsibility: Ingress payload reaches ConvEngine with user text, conversation id, and input params.

Session Mutations: userText, inputParams, bootstrap context placeholders.

Config/Table Dependencies: ce_conversation (R/W), ce_audit (W).

  • Code
  • Detailed Execution Logic
  • Tables Involved
ConversationController.message()
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/api/controller/ConversationController.java
JAVA
public ResponseEntity<ConversationResponse> message(@RequestBody ConversationRequest request) {
return ResponseEntity.ok(conversationalEngine.process(request));
}
DefaultConversationalEngine.process()
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/provider/DefaultConversationalEngine.java
JAVA
public ConversationResponse process(ConversationRequest request) {
EngineSession session = sessionManager.loadOrCreate(request);
enginePipelineFactory.getPipeline().execute(session);
return session.toResponse();
}
  1. HTTP payload is converted into an EngineSession with immutable conversation identity and mutable turn context.
  2. userText, optional inputParams, and request metadata are attached before any classifier or rule executes.
  3. Session bootstrap ensures downstream steps read and mutate a shared context map.
  4. Conversation state is loaded if conversation id exists; otherwise first-turn bootstrap is created.
  5. Audit context is opened so stage-level determinants are linked to this turn.

Step 1 table touch points

Table / sourceAccess pattern
HTTP body (R)Reads userText, conversationId, inputParams.
ce_conversation (R/W)Load existing conversation or bootstrap a new one.
ce_audit (W)Ingress lifecycle traces.
2McpToolStep + McpPlanner

Responsibility: Planner resolves semantic turn to CALL_TOOL with tool_code=db.semantic.query.

Session Mutations: context.mcp.lifecycle.*, MCP_ACTION, MCP_TOOL_CODE, planner observations.

Config/Table Dependencies: ce_mcp_planner (R), ce_mcp_tool (R), ce_rule (R), ce_audit (W).

  • Code
  • Detailed Execution Logic
  • Tables Involved
McpToolStep.execute()
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/steps/McpToolStep.java
JAVA
for (int i = 0; i < maxLoops; i++) {
McpPlan plan = planner.plan(session, tools, observations);
if (McpConstants.ACTION_ANSWER.equalsIgnoreCase(plan.action())) {
  writeFinalAnswerToContext(session, plan.answer());
  break;
}
CeMcpTool tool = registry.requireTool(plan.tool_code(), session.getIntent(), session.getState());
String rowsJson = resolveExecutor(toolGroup).execute(tool, args, session);
observations.add(new McpObservation(toolCode, rowsJson));
}
McpPlanner.plan()
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/McpPlanner.java
JAVA
PromptTemplateContext ctx = PromptTemplateContext.builder()
.templateName("McpPlanner")
.mcpTools(toolsJson)
.mcpObservations(obsJson)
.build();
String planJson = llm.generateJsonStrict(...);
return normalizePlan(mapper.readValue(planJson, McpPlan.class));
  1. McpToolStep runs PRE_AGENT_MCP rules so semantic flow can be state-gated.
  2. Enabled MCP tools are fetched for current intent and state.
  3. McpPlanner receives tool list + observations + context and emits strict JSON action.
  4. On CALL_TOOL, selected tool executes and observation is appended into MCP context.
  5. Loop continues until planner returns ANSWER, then mcp.finalAnswer is persisted.

Step 2 table touch points

Table / sourceAccess pattern
ce_mcp_planner (R)Planner prompt rows.
ce_mcp_tool (R)Tool registry + scope checks.
ce_rule (R)PRE/POST MCP transitions.
ce_audit (W)Planner and MCP lifecycle traces.
3DbSemanticQueryToolHandler

Responsibility: Bridge MCP DB tool invocation to semantic runtime.

Session Mutations: Question normalized from args/session.

Config/Table Dependencies: convengine.mcp.db.semantic.*, ce_audit (W).

  • Code
  • Detailed Execution Logic
  • Tables Involved
DbSemanticQueryToolHandler.toolCode()
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/runtime/handler/DbSemanticQueryToolHandler.java
JAVA
public String toolCode() {
ConvEngineMcpConfig.Db.Semantic cfg = ...;
return cfg.getToolCode() == null || cfg.getToolCode().isBlank()
    ? "db.semantic.query" : cfg.getToolCode();
}
DbSemanticQueryToolHandler.execute()
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/runtime/handler/DbSemanticQueryToolHandler.java
JAVA
public Object execute(CeMcpTool tool, Map<String, Object> args, EngineSession session) {
String question = extractQuestion(args, session);
return runtimeService.plan(question, session);
}
  1. Handler is resolved by MCP DB executor by tool code.
  2. Question extraction order is deterministic: question -> query -> user_input -> session.userText.
  3. Handler intentionally avoids planner/retrieval/SQL logic; it is an adapter boundary.
  4. Delegates to SemanticQueryRuntimeService.plan with normalized question.
  5. This keeps runtime swappable without changing MCP core contracts.

Step 3 table touch points

Table / sourceAccess pattern
ce_audit (W)Tool-handler entry traces.
4SemanticQueryRuntimeService

Responsibility: Orchestrate semantic stage pipeline and handle runtime errors.

Session Mutations: retrieval, joinPath, ast, canonicalAst, compiledSql, execution, summary, _meta, _db.

Config/Table Dependencies: mode and enable flags, ce_audit (W), ce_verbose (R).

  • Code
  • Detailed Execution Logic
  • Tables Involved
SemanticQueryRuntimeService.plan()
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/runtime/core/SemanticQueryRuntimeService.java
JAVA
for (SemanticQueryStage stage : stagePipeline.stages()) {
if (stage == null || !stage.supports(context)) continue;
publishBefore(stageInterceptors, stage.stageCode(), session, context);
try {
  stage.execute(context);
  publishAfter(stageInterceptors, stage.stageCode(), session, context);
} catch (Exception stageError) {
  publishStageError(stage, context, session, stageError);
  throw stageError;
}
}
SemanticStagePipelineFactory.init()
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/runtime/pipeline/SemanticStagePipelineFactory.java
JAVA
List<SemanticQueryStage> ordered = dagOrderer.order(
discoveredStages,
SemanticQueryStage.class,
"Semantic query stage pipeline",
c -> false
);
this.pipeline = new SemanticStagePipeline(ordered);
  1. Runtime rejects call unless mcp.db.query.mode=semantic and semantic is enabled.
  2. Interceptors are sorted once and wrapped around each stage.
  3. DAG-ordered stages execute deterministically.
  4. Stage failures emit stage-specific error determinants with rich metadata.
  5. Final output contains stage artifacts for troubleshooting and UI trace rendering.

Step 4 table touch points

Table / sourceAccess pattern
ce_audit (W)Stage enter/exit/meta/error determinants.
ce_verbose (R)Determinant message lookup.
5SemanticRetrievalStage

Responsibility: Retrieve semantic entity and candidate tables.

Session Mutations: retrieval.candidateEntities, retrieval.candidateTables, confidence.

Config/Table Dependencies: semantic-layer.yaml, optional embeddings/vector search, ce_audit.

  • Code
  • Detailed Execution Logic
  • Tables Involved
SemanticRetrievalStage.execute()
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/runtime/stage/provider/SemanticRetrievalStage.java
JAVA
SemanticEntityTableRetriever retriever = resolveRetriever(context);
RetrievalResult retrieval = retriever.retrieve(context.question(), context.session());
context.retrieval(retrieval);
DefaultSemanticEntityTableRetriever scoring
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/retrieval/provider/DefaultSemanticEntityTableRetriever.java
JAVA
double deterministic = rc.getSynonymWeight() * synonym
+ rc.getFieldWeight() * fields
+ rc.getIdPatternWeight() * idPattern
+ rc.getLexicalWeight() * lexical;
double total = rc.getDeterministicBlendWeight() * clamp01(deterministic)
+ rc.getVectorBlendWeight() * clamp01(vector);

Retrieval is the stage that decides which semantic entity and tables the rest of the pipeline should trust. Everything downstream (join-path, AST quality, SQL compile quality) depends on this ranking.

Retrieval behavior: without vector vs with vector

ModeWhat gets computedStrengthWeakness
No vector (`mcp.db.semantic.vector.enabled=false`)Deterministic lexical scoring only: synonym overlap, field overlap, id-pattern boosts, description overlap.Very predictable and easy to debug.Can miss semantic paraphrases when words differ from YAML wording.
Vector enabled (`mcp.db.semantic.vector.enabled=true`)Deterministic lexical + embedding similarity from `ce_mcp_semantic_embedding` (`ENTITY` and `TABLE`).Better semantic recall for paraphrases and natural language variations.Needs embedding rows and model quality; slightly more moving parts.

Activation YAML

Enable vector retrieval for semantic query
YAML
mcp:
  db:
    query:
      mode: semantic
    semantic:
      enabled: true
      model-path: classpath:/mcp/semantic-layer.yaml
      vector:
        enabled: true
        feedback-enabled: true
        table: ce_mcp_semantic_embedding
        namespace-column: namespace
        target-type-column: target_type
        target-name-column: target_name
        embedding-column: embedding
        metadata-column: metadata_json
        max-results: 20

Behind-the-scenes workflow (no vector)

  1. Resolve retriever bean via @Order + supports(session).
  2. Tokenize question to normalized lowercase terms.
  3. For each semantic entity: compute deterministic score using synonyms + fields + lexical + id-pattern.
  4. For selected entities, score candidate tables using table-name + field-column lexical overlap.
  5. Apply thresholds + top-N limits and write retrieval.candidateEntities and retrieval.candidateTables.

Behind-the-scenes workflow (vector enabled)

  1. Everything from no-vector mode still runs.
  2. Generate embedding for user question using llmClient.generateEmbedding(question).
  3. Run pgvector lookup via PostgresPgVectorSearchAdapter for nearest ENTITY and TABLE rows.
  4. Blend deterministic score and vector score per candidate.
  5. If feedback vectors are enabled, apply additional USER_QUERY similarity boost (feedbackVector).
  6. Sort final blended scores and output final candidate lists.

How lexical matching works (with your semantic YAML)

YAML features that lexical retrieval reads
YAML
entities:
  DisconnectRequest:
    synonyms:
      - disconnect
      - disconnection
      - service termination
    fields:
      requestId:
        column: zp_request.zp_request_id
        description: unique request id
      downstreamStatus:
        column: zp_disconnect_order.downstream_status
        description: downstream orchestration status

Lexical scoring signals

SignalSource in YAML/questionExample
Synonym overlapquestion tokens vs `entities.*.synonyms`Question has `termination` -> matches `service termination` synonym.
Field overlapquestion tokens vs field keys + field descriptions + column namesQuestion has `downstream status` -> matches `downstreamStatus` field.
Description lexical overlapquestion tokens vs entity description textQuestion has `failed disconnect` -> overlaps entity description words.
ID pattern boostsregex in question (`ZPR*`, `DON*`, `ZPDISC*`)`DON9001` boosts disconnect/order-related entity.

Score blending concept

Entity/table ranking uses weighted blending from config.

Retrieval score blend (conceptual)
JAVA
double deterministic = synonymWeight * synonym
+ fieldWeight * fields
+ idPatternWeight * idPattern
+ lexicalWeight * lexical;

double total = deterministicBlendWeight * clamp01(deterministic)
+ vectorBlendWeight * clamp01(vectorScore)
+ feedbackWeight * clamp01(feedbackVectorScore);

Where each score comes from

ScoreProduced byUsed when
deterministicDefaultSemanticEntityTableRetriever lexical/id scoringAlways
vectorScorePostgresPgVectorSearchAdapter over `ce_mcp_semantic_embedding` (`ENTITY`,`TABLE`)When `vector.enabled=true` and embedding exists
feedbackVectorScoreNearest `USER_QUERY` vectors from feedback memoryWhen `vector.enabled=true` and `vector.feedback-enabled=true`

Turn-by-turn mini example

Example: same question, without and with vector

TurnUser inputNo-vector likely outcomeVector-enabled likely outcome
T1why termination failed for DON9001May rely on lexical `failed` + `DON9001`; if `termination` token is weak in YAML, ranking can be medium confidence.Vector similarity pulls `DisconnectRequest`/`zp_disconnect_order` higher because `termination` semantically matches disconnect domain.
T2show downstream status and team notesLexical may still find correct fields, but ranking can vary if wording differs.Vector + lexical stabilizes candidate entity/tables and improves consistency of selected fields.
T3list failed requests from last 24hGood lexical hit, usually correct.Also correct; vector mainly helps robustness for alternate phrasing.

Key point: Retrieval is a ranking stage. Vector does not replace lexical logic; it augments it to improve semantic recall. The output from this stage (candidateEntities, candidateTables, reasons, confidence) is the contract consumed by join-path and AST stages.

Step 5 table touch points

Table / sourceAccess pattern
semantic-layer.yaml (R)Entity metadata, synonyms, fields.
pgvector (optional R)Vector similarity lookup.
ce_audit (W)RETRIEVAL_DONE determinant.
6SemanticJoinPathStage

Responsibility: Resolve join path using schema graph traversal.

Session Mutations: joinPath.baseTable, joinPath.edges, joinPath.requiredTables, joinPath.unresolvedTables.

Config/Table Dependencies: graph adapter (jgrapht), DB FK metadata, ce_audit.

  • Code
  • Detailed Execution Logic
  • Tables Involved
DefaultJoinPathResolver.doResolve()
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/graph/provider/DefaultJoinPathResolver.java
JAVA
String base = candidates.get(0).name();
for (String target : requiredTables) {
if (base.equalsIgnoreCase(target)) continue;
List<SchemaEdge> path = graph.shortestPath(base, target, maxHops);
if (path == null || path.isEmpty()) unresolved.add(target);
}
JGraphTSchemaGraphEngine.shortestPath()
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/graph/provider/JGraphTSchemaGraphEngine.java
JAVA
while (!queue.isEmpty()) {
NodePath current = queue.poll();
if (current.node().equals(toTable)) return toEdges(current.path());
for (String next : neighbors(current.node())) {
  if (!visited.contains(next)) queue.add(new NodePath(next, nextPath));
}
}
  1. Base table is picked from top candidate table from retrieval stage.
  2. Required tables are resolved from selected candidates.
  3. Shortest-path traversal is run per target table under maxJoinHops.
  4. Resolved edges are merged without duplicates.
  5. Unresolved tables are captured and later enforced by AST validator.

Step 6 table touch points

Table / sourceAccess pattern
FK metadata via schema inspector (R)Graph vertex/edge construction.
semantic-layer relationships (R)Join expectations and hints.
ce_audit (W)JOIN_PATH_RESOLVED determinant.
7SemanticAstGenerationStage

Responsibility: Generate strict JSON AST using LLM.

Session Mutations: astRawJson, astVersion, SemanticQueryAstV1, canonicalAst.

Config/Table Dependencies: ce_config prompt keys for DefaultSemanticAstGenerator, ce_audit.

  • Code
  • Detailed Execution Logic
  • Tables Involved
SemanticAstGenerationStage.execute()
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/runtime/stage/provider/SemanticAstGenerationStage.java
JAVA
AstGenerationResult generated = generator.generate(
context.question(), context.retrieval(), context.joinPath(), context.session());
context.astGeneration(generated);
if (generated != null && generated.ast() != null) {
context.canonicalAst(astCanonicalizer.fromV1(generated.ast()));
}
DefaultSemanticAstGenerator.doGenerate()
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/llm/DefaultSemanticAstGenerator.java
JAVA
String raw = llmClient.generateJsonStrict(session, llmPrompt, jsonSchema, ctxJson);
SemanticQueryAstV1 ast = mapper.readValue(raw, SemanticQueryAstV1.class);
ast = astNormalizer.normalize(ast, model, entity, session);
publishLlmEvent(ConvEngineAuditStage.AST_OUTPUT.name(), session, false, llmOutputPayload);
  1. Stage runs only after retrieval + join-path success.
  2. Prompt context includes question, selected entity, allowed entities, candidate tables, join path, and context json.
  3. Schema JSON is entity-specialized so generated fields stay within selected semantic entity.
  4. LLM output is strict JSON; SQL text output is not accepted in this stage.
  5. Raw JSON is normalized and audited via AST_INPUT, AST_OUTPUT, AST_ERROR determinants.

Step 7 table touch points

Table / sourceAccess pattern
ce_config (R)SYSTEM_PROMPT / USER_PROMPT / SCHEMA_PROMPT.
ce_audit (W)AST_INPUT / AST_OUTPUT / AST_ERROR.
ce_verbose (R)Verbose determinant labels.
8SemanticAstValidationStage

Responsibility: Validate canonical AST against semantic contracts.

Session Mutations: astValidation.valid, astValidation.errors.

Config/Table Dependencies: semantic model fields/metrics/rules, ce_audit.

  • Code
  • Detailed Execution Logic
  • Tables Involved
SemanticAstValidationStage.execute()
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/runtime/stage/provider/SemanticAstValidationStage.java
JAVA
AstValidationResult result = validator.validate(
context.canonicalAst(), model, context.joinPath(), context.session());
context.astValidation(result);
if (!result.valid()) {
throw new IllegalStateException("semantic AST validation failed: " + result.errors());
}
DefaultAstValidator key checks
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/ast/validate/DefaultAstValidator.java
JAVA
validateExistsBlocks(errors, ast.existsBlocks(), model);
validateSubqueryFilters(errors, ast.subqueryFilters(), fieldNames, model);
validateWindows(errors, ast.windows(), fieldNames);
if (ast.limit() > maxLimit) errors.add("limit exceeds max: " + ast.limit());
  1. Entity must exist in semantic model; unknown entity fails immediately.
  2. Projection/group/sort/filter field keys are validated against semantic fields.
  3. Operator-value shape is validated (IN, BETWEEN, null-check operators, etc.).
  4. Exists/subquery/window clauses are recursively validated.
  5. Unresolved join-path tables and limit/offset constraints are enforced pre-compile.

Step 8 table touch points

Table / sourceAccess pattern
semantic-layer.yaml (R)Entity/field/operator/metric contract.
ce_audit (W)AST_VALIDATED determinant.
9SemanticSqlCompileStage

Responsibility: Deterministic AST -> parameterized SQL compile + policy validation.

Session Mutations: compiledSql, compiledSqlParams, sqlCompiled=true.

Config/Table Dependencies: semantic model mappings, SQL clause/operator/window handlers, McpSqlGuardrail, ce_audit.

  • Code
  • Detailed Execution Logic
  • Tables Involved
SemanticSqlCompileStage.execute()
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/runtime/stage/provider/SemanticSqlCompileStage.java
JAVA
CompiledSql compiled = compiler.compile(context);
resolveValidator(context).validate(compiled, context);
context.compiledSql(compiled);
publishCompiled(context.session(), compiled);
DefaultSemanticSqlCompiler.compile()
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/sql/compiler/DefaultSemanticSqlCompiler.java
JAVA
CompileWorkPlan plan = new CompileWorkPlan(context, ast, model, entity, context.joinPath());
clauseHandlerRegistry.applyAll(plan);
return plan.getCompiledSql();
DefaultSemanticSqlPolicyValidator.validate()
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/sql/policy/DefaultSemanticSqlPolicyValidator.java
JAVA
sqlGuardrail.assertReadOnly(compiledSql.sql(), "semantic.query");
  1. Compile stage runs only after AST validation is successful.
  2. Compiler uses canonical AST and semantic model to resolve physical columns/tables.
  3. Clause handler registries apply predicates, metrics, windows, exists, and subqueries.
  4. Output SQL is always parameterized with named bind values.
  5. Policy validator applies read-only guardrail before execution stage.

Step 9 table touch points

Table / sourceAccess pattern
semantic-layer.yaml (R)Field-to-column and metric mappings.
ce_audit (W)SQL_COMPILED determinant.
10SemanticSqlExecuteStage

Responsibility: Execute compiled SQL and map result rows.

Session Mutations: execution.rowCount, execution.rows[], _db.sql, _db.params.

Config/Table Dependencies: datasource/JDBC, domain tables (read-only), ce_audit.

  • Code
  • Detailed Execution Logic
  • Tables Involved
SemanticSqlExecuteStage.execute()
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/runtime/stage/provider/SemanticSqlExecuteStage.java
JAVA
SemanticSqlExecutor executor = resolveExecutor(context);
SemanticExecutionResult result = executor.execute(context.compiledSql(), context);
context.executionResult(result);
publishExecuted(context, result);
DefaultSemanticSqlExecutor.execute()
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/execute/DefaultSemanticSqlExecutor.java
JAVA
List<Map<String, Object>> rows = jdbcTemplate.queryForList(
compiledSql.sql(),
compiledSql.params() == null ? Map.of() : compiledSql.params()
);
return new SemanticExecutionResult(rows.size(), List.copyOf(rows));
  1. Stage fails fast if SQL compile output is missing.
  2. Named-parameter SQL executes through NamedParameterJdbcTemplate.
  3. Result rows are copied into immutable execution payload.
  4. Audit payload captures SQL and bind params under _db for debugging.
  5. DB grammar/type/permission errors are surfaced as SQL_EXECUTE_ERROR with root cause.

Step 10 table touch points

Table / sourceAccess pattern
zapper transactional tables (R)SELECT execution only.
ce_audit (W)SQL_EXECUTED/SQL_EXECUTE_ERROR.
11SemanticResultSummaryStage

Responsibility: Build deterministic text/markdown summary from result rows.

Session Mutations: summary.

Config/Table Dependencies: execution result data, ce_audit.

  • Code
  • Detailed Execution Logic
  • Tables Involved
SemanticResultSummaryStage.execute()
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/runtime/stage/provider/SemanticResultSummaryStage.java
JAVA
SemanticResultSummarizer summarizer = resolveSummarizer(context);
String summary = summarizer.summarize(context.executionResult(), context);
context.summary(summary);
DefaultSemanticResultSummarizer.summarize()
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/summary/DefaultSemanticResultSummarizer.java
JAVA
if (result.rowCount() == 0) return "No matching records found.";
return buildMarkdownTable(result);
  1. No-result branch returns deterministic string, not LLM-generated prose.
  2. Result-table branch extracts headers from first row keys.
  3. Rows are serialized as markdown table to render in ConvEngine UI/docs trace.
  4. Cell escape logic keeps table valid for symbols/newlines.
  5. Summary preview + length are emitted into audit payload.

Step 11 table touch points

Table / sourceAccess pattern
execution.rows (R)Summary source rows.
ce_audit (W)RESULT_SUMMARIZED.
12McpPlanner + RulesStep (POST_AGENT_MCP)

Responsibility: Planner finalizes answer and rules complete post-MCP transitions.

Session Mutations: mcp.finalAnswer, outcome/status flags, state transitions.

Config/Table Dependencies: ce_mcp_planner, ce_rule, ce_audit.

  • Code
  • Detailed Execution Logic
  • Tables Involved
McpToolStep ANSWER branch
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/steps/McpToolStep.java
JAVA
if (McpConstants.ACTION_ANSWER.equalsIgnoreCase(plan.action())) {
writeFinalAnswerToContext(session, plan.answer());
session.putInputParam(ConvEngineInputParamKey.MCP_FINAL_ANSWER, plan.answer());
break;
}
RulesStep.applyRules(...)
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/steps/RulesStep.java
JAVA
String phase = RulePhase.normalize(requestedPhase);
List<CeRule> allRules = staticCacheService.findEligibleRules(passIntent, passState, phase);
actionResolver.resolve(session, rule);
  1. After semantic execution, planner receives fresh observations and decides final answer text.
  2. mcp.finalAnswer is stored in context and input params.
  3. POST_AGENT_MCP rule phase runs to move workflow state.
  4. Rule actions can map to success/failure/reset states.
  5. Planner and rule outcomes are auditable for RCA.

Step 12 table touch points

Table / sourceAccess pattern
ce_mcp_planner (R)Answer synthesis prompt.
ce_rule (R)POST_AGENT_MCP transitions.
ce_audit (W)Planner + rule outcomes.
13ResponseResolutionStep + Assistant output

Responsibility: Resolve final response mapping and emit assistant payload.

Session Mutations: OutputPayload + persisted turn output.

Config/Table Dependencies: ce_response, ce_prompt_template, ce_output_schema (optional), ce_conversation, ce_audit.

  • Code
  • Detailed Execution Logic
  • Tables Involved
ResponseResolutionStep.execute()
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/steps/ResponseResolutionStep.java
JAVA
Optional<CeResponse> responseOptional = resolveResponse(session);
CeResponse resp = responseOptional.get();
typeFactory.get(resp.getResponseType(), session)
.resolve(session, PromptTemplate.initFrom(template), ResponseTemplate.initFrom(resp));
ResponseResolutionStep assistant audit
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/steps/ResponseResolutionStep.java
JAVA
outputPayload.put(ConvEnginePayloadKey.OUTPUT, payloadValue);
outputPayload.put(ConvEnginePayloadKey.OUTPUT_FORMAT, resp.getOutputFormat());
audit.audit(ConvEngineAuditStage.ASSISTANT_OUTPUT, session.getConversationId(), outputPayload);
  1. Response row is selected by intent/state and priority.
  2. Response type handler renders EXACT/DERIVED payload accordingly.
  3. Output transformers are applied if configured.
  4. Final payload is attached to session and returned as assistant turn output.
  5. Assistant output determinant closes the turn-level audit trail.

Step 13 table touch points

Table / sourceAccess pattern
ce_response (R)Intent/state response mapping.
ce_prompt_template (R)Template rows for DERIVED/SCHEMA-based response.
ce_output_schema (R optional)Output schema helper context.
ce_conversation (W)Persisted final turn.
ce_audit (W)ASSISTANT_OUTPUT.

Semantic Model YAML (semantic-layer.yaml)​

Semantic mode relies on a declarative semantic model file that maps business language to physical schema. Retrieval, AST validation, join-path planning, metric compilation, and guardrails all consume this file.

YAML section ownership

SectionPrimary consumersRuntime effect
settingsretrieval + compilerdefault limits, timezone, SQL dialect hints
entitiesretrieval + validator + compilerbusiness entities, synonyms, canonical fields
tablesgraph/planner/compilerphysical columns and foreign keys
relationshipsjoin-path stagetable traversal edges and cardinality hints
join_hintsplanner/compilerfrequent join relationships
metricsvalidator + compilerallowed metric keys and SQL expressions
value_patternsnormalizerglobal value-prefix-driven field remap before validation/compile
synonymsretrievaldomain query vocabulary expansion
rulespolicy/guardrailallowed tables, denied operations, max limit
Configured path

mcp.db.semantic.model-path=classpath:/mcp/semantic-layer.yaml

Complete sample semantic model​

semantic-layer.yaml (full sample)
YAML
version: 1
database: zapper_ops
description: >
Semantic model for Zapper disconnect workflow analytics and production diagnostics.
This model maps user/business language (disconnect, downstream failure, BillBank gap,
account status) to physical database tables and join paths.

settings:
  default_limit: 100
  timezone: UTC
  sql_dialect: postgres

entities:
  Account:
    description: >
      Represents a Zapper customer billing account used as the anchor for request,
      order, and billing diagnostics.
    synonyms:
      - contract
      - subscription
      - service account
      - customer account
      - billing account
    example_questions:
      - show account details for UPSA100
      - what is account status for ACMA200
      - list accounts created this week
      - show billing cost for account UPSA100
    tables:
      primary: zp_account
      related:
        - zp_customer
    fields:
      accountId:
        column: zp_account.account_id
        type: string
        description: unique account identifier used across request/order/billing tables
        searchable: true
        filterable: true
        example: UPSA100
      customerId:
        column: zp_account.customer_id
        type: string
        description: owner customer identifier linked to zp_customer
        searchable: true
        filterable: true
      accountStatus:
        column: zp_account.account_status
        type: string
        description: account lifecycle status in Zapper
        filterable: true
        allowed_values:
          - ACTIVE
          - SUSPENDED
          - CLOSED
      currentBillingCost:
        column: zp_account.current_billing_cost
        type: number
        description: current computed billing cost amount for account
      createdAt:
        column: zp_account.created_at
        type: timestamp
        description: account creation timestamp

DisconnectRequest:
  description: >
    Represents disconnect workflow requests and downstream order execution state.
    This is the main entity for "why disconnect failed" style analysis.
  synonyms:
    - disconnect
    - disconnection
    - service termination
    - cancel service
    - disconnect request
    - failed disconnect
  example_questions:
    - show failed disconnect requests last 24 hours
    - why disconnect failed for request ZPR1003
    - list disconnect requests for account UPSA100
    - show request status and downstream status together
    - find requests where disconnect id is missing
  tables:
    primary: zp_request
    related:
      - zp_disconnect_order
      - zp_order_downstream_check
      - zp_ui_data
  fields:
    requestId:
      column: zp_request.zp_request_id
      type: string
      key: true
      searchable: true
      filterable: true
      aliases:
        - id
        - request id
        - req id
      description: unique request id (example pattern ZPR*)
      example: ZPR1003
    accountId:
      column: zp_request.account_id
      type: string
      searchable: true
      filterable: true
      description: account associated with request
    requestStatus:
      column: zp_request.request_status
      type: string
      filterable: true
      aliases:
        - status
        - request state
      description: request-level status from orchestration layer
      allowed_values:
        - SUBMITTED
        - IN_PROGRESS
        - FAILED
        - COMPLETED
    requestedAt:
      column: zp_request.requested_at
      type: timestamp
      aliases:
        - created
        - created at
        - request time
      description: request timestamp used for time-window diagnostics
    disconnectOrderId:
      column: zp_disconnect_order.don
      type: string
      searchable: true
      filterable: true
      description: downstream disconnect order number (DON*)
      example: DON9001
    downstreamStatus:
      column: zp_disconnect_order.downstream_status
      type: string
      filterable: true
      description: downstream orchestration status for disconnect order
      allowed_values:
        - COMPLETED
        - FAILED
        - PENDING
        - INVENTORY_ERROR
    disconnectId:
      column: zp_disconnect_order.zp_disconnect_id
      type: string
      searchable: true
      filterable: true
      description: generated disconnect identifier, can be null during failures
    teamNotes:
      column: zp_ui_data.zp_asr_team_notes
      type: string
      description: latest operations team note useful for RCA context
    failureReason:
      column: zp_ui_data.zp_asr_team_notes
      type: string
      filterable: true
      searchable: true
      description: normalized failure-reason view for request-level RCA prompts

DownstreamCheck:
  description: >
    Represents validation checks executed in downstream disconnect flow.
    Used to diagnose which specific check failed and why.
  synonyms:
    - check
    - validation check
    - downstream validation
    - downstream rule
    - check failure
  example_questions:
    - list failed checks for DON9001
    - which check failed for request ZPR1003
    - show failure reason for request ZPR1003
    - show downstream validation failures last 24 hours
  tables:
    primary: zp_order_downstream_check
    related:
      - zp_disconnect_order
  fields:
    checkId:
      column: zp_order_downstream_check.check_id
      type: string
      key: true
      searchable: true
      description: unique check record identifier
    disconnectOrderId:
      column: zp_order_downstream_check.don
      type: string
      filterable: true
      searchable: true
      description: disconnect order linked to this check
    requestId:
      column: zp_disconnect_order.zp_request_id
      type: string
      filterable: true
      searchable: true
      description: request id associated with this downstream check via disconnect order
    checkCode:
      column: zp_order_downstream_check.check_code
      type: string
      filterable: true
      description: business check code executed downstream
    checkStatus:
      column: zp_order_downstream_check.check_status
      type: string
      filterable: true
      description: result of downstream validation check
      allowed_values:
        - PASSED
        - FAILED
        - SKIPPED
    failureReason:
      column: zp_order_downstream_check.failure_reason
      type: string
      description: captured failure reason when checkStatus=FAILED
    checkedAt:
      column: zp_order_downstream_check.checked_at
      type: timestamp
      description: time check was executed

BillingRecord:
  description: >
    Represents BillBank/billing records associated with disconnect outcomes.
    Used to identify billing reconciliation gaps after disconnect.
  synonyms:
    - billing
    - billbank
    - invoice
    - bill record
    - billing reconciliation
  example_questions:
    - show billbank record for disconnect id ZPDISC1001
    - list pending billing records for account UPSA100
    - find disconnects missing billbank record
  tables:
    primary: zp_billbank_record
    related:
      - zp_disconnect_order
  fields:
    billbankId:
      column: zp_billbank_record.billbank_id
      type: string
      key: true
      searchable: true
      description: BillBank record identifier
    accountId:
      column: zp_billbank_record.account_id
      type: string
      filterable: true
      searchable: true
      description: account tied to billing record
    disconnectId:
      column: zp_billbank_record.zp_disconnect_id
      type: string
      filterable: true
      searchable: true
      description: disconnect identifier linked to billing record
    recordStatus:
      column: zp_billbank_record.record_status
      type: string
      filterable: true
      description: BillBank processing status
      allowed_values:
        - POSTED
        - PENDING
        - FAILED
    pendingBillAmount:
      column: zp_billbank_record.pending_bill_amount
      type: number
      description: pending bill amount for account/disconnect
    updatedAt:
      column: zp_billbank_record.updated_at
      type: timestamp
      description: record last update timestamp

tables:
  zp_account:
    description: account master data
    columns:
      account_id:
        type: varchar
        primary_key: true
        description: unique account identifier
      customer_id:
        type: varchar
        description: customer owner id
        foreign_key:
          table: zp_customer
          column: customer_id
      account_status:
        type: varchar
        description: account lifecycle status
      current_billing_cost:
        type: numeric
        description: current billing amount
      created_at:
        type: timestamptz
        description: account creation time

zp_customer:
  description: customer master data
  columns:
    customer_id:
      type: varchar
      primary_key: true
    customer_name:
      type: varchar

zp_request:
  description: disconnect request master table
  columns:
    zp_request_id:
      type: varchar
      primary_key: true
    account_id:
      type: varchar
      foreign_key:
        table: zp_account
        column: account_id
    request_status:
      type: varchar
    requested_at:
      type: timestamptz

zp_disconnect_order:
  description: downstream disconnect order tracking
  columns:
    don:
      type: varchar
      primary_key: true
    zp_request_id:
      type: varchar
      foreign_key:
        table: zp_request
        column: zp_request_id
    account_id:
      type: varchar
      foreign_key:
        table: zp_account
        column: account_id
    downstream_status:
      type: varchar
    zp_disconnect_id:
      type: varchar

zp_order_downstream_check:
  description: downstream validation checks
  columns:
    check_id:
      type: varchar
      primary_key: true
    don:
      type: varchar
      foreign_key:
        table: zp_disconnect_order
        column: don
    check_code:
      type: varchar
    check_status:
      type: varchar
    failure_reason:
      type: varchar
    checked_at:
      type: timestamptz

zp_ui_data:
  description: latest UI operations notes per request
  columns:
    zp_request_id:
      type: varchar
      primary_key: true
      foreign_key:
        table: zp_request
        column: zp_request_id
    zp_asr_team_notes:
      type: varchar

zp_billbank_record:
  description: bill-bank records for disconnect and cease state
  columns:
    billbank_id:
      type: varchar
      primary_key: true
    account_id:
      type: varchar
      foreign_key:
        table: zp_account
        column: account_id
    zp_disconnect_id:
      type: varchar
    record_status:
      type: varchar
    pending_bill_amount:
      type: numeric
    updated_at:
      type: timestamptz

relationships:
  - name: account_to_request
    description: an account can have many disconnect requests
    from:
      table: zp_account
      column: account_id
    to:
      table: zp_request
      column: account_id
    type: one_to_many

- name: request_to_disconnect_order
  description: request to downstream disconnect order
  from:
    table: zp_request
    column: zp_request_id
  to:
    table: zp_disconnect_order
    column: zp_request_id
  type: one_to_many

- name: request_to_ui_data
  description: request to latest UI note payload
  from:
    table: zp_request
    column: zp_request_id
  to:
    table: zp_ui_data
    column: zp_request_id
  type: one_to_one

- name: disconnect_order_to_check
  description: disconnect order to downstream checks
  from:
    table: zp_disconnect_order
    column: don
  to:
    table: zp_order_downstream_check
    column: don
  type: one_to_many

- name: account_to_billbank
  description: account to billbank records
  from:
    table: zp_account
    column: account_id
  to:
    table: zp_billbank_record
    column: account_id
  type: one_to_many

- name: disconnect_to_billbank
  description: disconnect id mapped to billbank records when generated
  from:
    table: zp_disconnect_order
    column: zp_disconnect_id
  to:
    table: zp_billbank_record
    column: zp_disconnect_id
  type: one_to_many

synonyms:
  account:
    - contract
    - subscription
    - billing account
    - customer account
  disconnect:
    - service termination
    - cancel service
    - disconnection
    - cease service
  downstream:
    - order check
    - validation
    - orchestration
  billing:
    - billbank
    - invoice
    - reconciliation

join_hints:
  zp_request:
    commonly_joined_with:
      - zp_disconnect_order
      - zp_ui_data
      - zp_account
      - zp_order_downstream_check

zp_disconnect_order:
  commonly_joined_with:
    - zp_request
    - zp_order_downstream_check
    - zp_billbank_record

metrics:
  failed_disconnect_count:
    description: count of disconnect requests with non-completed downstream status
    sql: |
      COUNT(CASE WHEN zp_disconnect_order.downstream_status NOT IN ('COMPLETED') THEN 1 END)

pending_disconnect_count:
  description: count of disconnect requests still pending downstream completion
  sql: |
    COUNT(CASE WHEN zp_disconnect_order.downstream_status IN ('PENDING') THEN 1 END)

billbank_gap_count:
  description: count of disconnect orders missing billbank linkage
  sql: |
    COUNT(CASE WHEN zp_disconnect_order.zp_disconnect_id IS NOT NULL
               AND zp_billbank_record.zp_disconnect_id IS NULL THEN 1 END)

value_patterns:
  - from_field: requestId
    to_field: disconnectOrderId
    value_starts_with: [DON]
  - from_field: disconnectOrderId
    to_field: requestId
    value_starts_with: [ZPR]

intent_rules:
  don_failure_reason_details:
    description: stabilize DON-level failure asks to disconnect-order diagnostics output
    match_any: [why did, fail, failed, failure reason]
    must_contain: [don]
    force_entity: DisconnectRequest
    force_mode: detail
    force_select: [disconnectOrderId, downstreamStatus, failureReason, requestId]

request_failure_reason_details:
  description: stabilize request-level failure-reason asks to request diagnostics output
  match_any: [failure reason, reason for request, why request failed, failed reason]
  must_contain: [request]
  force_entity: DisconnectRequest
  force_mode: detail
  force_select: [requestId, failureReason]

billbank_gap_details:
  description: force billbank gap details flow
  match_any: [billbank gap, billbank gaps, billing gap, billing gaps]
  must_contain: [request, order, detail]
  force_entity: DisconnectRequest
  force_mode: detail
  force_mode_even_with_metrics: true
  force_select: [requestId, accountId, requestStatus, requestedAt, disconnectOrderId, downstreamStatus, disconnectId, teamNotes]
  enforce_where:
    - field: disconnectId
      op: IS_NULL
      value: null

rules:
  allowed_tables:
    - zp_account
    - zp_customer
    - zp_request
    - zp_disconnect_order
    - zp_order_downstream_check
    - zp_ui_data
    - zp_billbank_record
  deny_operations:
    - DELETE
    - UPDATE
    - DROP
  max_result_limit: 500

semantic-layer.yaml​

This section explains every major tag in the full sample and what ConvEngine actually does with it at runtime.

Important runtime behavior

SemanticModelLoader is configured with FAIL_ON_UNKNOWN_PROPERTIES=false.
That means unknown/unmapped YAML tags are ignored safely at load time.

Top-level tag behavior

TagLoaded into modelRuntime effectPrimary code path
versionYesReturned in output/audit as semanticModelVersion.model/SemanticModel, runtime/core/SemanticQueryRuntimeService
databaseYesInformational model identity.model/SemanticModel
descriptionYesUsed as descriptive context only.model/SemanticModel
entitiesYesCore retrieval + AST schema specialization + field validation + SQL compile mapping.retrieval/*, llm/DefaultSemanticAstGenerator, ast/validate/DefaultAstValidator, sql/*
tablesYesSchema graph and model validation for physical table references.model/SemanticModelValidator, graph/*
relationshipsYesJoin-path planning and correlation predicates.graph/provider/DefaultJoinPathResolver, sql/handler/clause/*
metricsYesAllowed metric keys and SQL expressions in aggregate queries.llm/DefaultSemanticAstGenerator, ast/validate/DefaultAstValidator, sql/handler/clause/*
value_patternsYesGlobal value-prefix remap rules applied before validation/compile (example DON* from requestId to disconnectOrderId).ast/normalize/DefaultAstNormalizer
intent_rulesYesDeterministic query-intent overrides (entity/mode/select/filters) applied during AST normalization.ast/normalize/DefaultAstNormalizer
synonymsYes (top-level map)Currently loaded; retrieval mainly uses entity-level synonyms.model/SemanticModel, retrieval/provider/DefaultSemanticEntityTableRetriever
settingsNo (ignored currently)No direct runtime effect from YAML tag itself; limits/timezone come from application config.config/ConvEngineMcpConfig
join_hintsNo (ignored currently)No direct runtime effect from YAML tag currently.N/A (ignored by loader)
rulesNo (ignored currently)No direct runtime effect from YAML tag currently.N/A (ignored by loader)

1) Model identity tags​

Snippet: model identity
YAML

What this does:

  • version: surfaces as semanticModelVersion in runtime output and stage payloads.
  • database + description: metadata/context only; not a SQL generator by itself.

2) entities block​

Snippet: one entity contract
YAML

How each child tag is used:

Entity tag behavior

TagUsed byEffect on AST/SQL
entities.<name>retrieval + validatorBecomes candidate entity and AST entity target.
descriptionretrieval + LLM promptImproves retrieval relevance and LLM grounding context.
synonymsretrieval (+ embedding text)Raises deterministic synonym score for candidate ranking.
tables.primaryplanner/compiler/validatorPrimary base table candidate for joins and projections.
tables.relatedretrieval/plannerExpands table candidates for join-path resolution.
fields.<fieldName>.columncompilerMaps semantic field key to physical table.column in SQL select/where/order/group.
fields.<fieldName>.typevalidatorOperator/type validation (e.g., ILIKE requires string-like field).
fields.<fieldName>.descriptionretrievalExtra lexical signal for entity/field matching.
fields.<fieldName>.aliasesnormalizer + LLM prompt contextMaps user/LLM variants (for example id/status/created) to canonical semantic field keys.
fields.<fieldName>.filterable/searchable/keycurrently metadataCarried in model; not strict query gating in current validator/compiler.

Example:

  • User asks: show requests where teamNotes contains billing
  • Retrieval picks DisconnectRequest (field ownership + lexical/synonym/vector signals).
  • AST schema specialization restricts allowed field keys for selected entity.
  • Compiler uses teamNotes -> zp_ui_data.zp_asr_team_notes and, if filters reference another table-owned field (for example requestId -> zp_request.zp_request_id), compile stage auto-expands required tables and adds the join path.

3) tables + columns + foreign_key​

Snippet: physical table dictionary
YAML

What this does:

  • Provides table/column metadata for schema validation and graph wiring.
  • primary_key and foreign_key are model-level structure hints.
  • foreign_key.table/column can contribute to relationship sanity and graph traversal completeness.

4) relationships​

Snippet: relationship edge
YAML

What this does:

  • Join-path stage builds traversal edges from these mappings.
  • SQL compiler uses edges to generate deterministic joins.
  • Correlation predicates for EXISTS/subqueries also rely on relationship knowledge.

5) metrics​

Snippet: metric definition
YAML

What this does:

  • Metric key becomes allowed value in AST metrics[].
  • Metric SQL is inserted by compiler when aggregation is requested.
  • Having clause can reference metric keys.

Critical rule:

  • If metrics is present with detail projections, selected fields must be grouped (group_by) or query is invalid.

6) synonyms (top-level)​

Snippet: top-level synonyms map
YAML

Current behavior:

  • Loaded into model and available for future expansion.
  • Main retrieval scoring today relies primarily on entity-level synonyms + fields + descriptions.

7) value_patterns + intent_rules (deterministic bridge for ambiguous asks)​

Snippet: intent rule for billbank gap details
YAML

What this does at runtime:

  • match_any + must_contain gates when rule activates.
  • force_entity overrides LLM-selected entity.
  • force_mode: detail drops accidental aggregate pieces (metrics, having, group_by).
  • force_mode_even_with_metrics: true allows this rule to force detail/select even if AST contains metrics.
  • force_select sets deterministic projection for that business intent.
  • enforce_where injects deterministic filters.
  • Global value_patterns applies deterministic filter-field remap based on value prefix (example requestId + DON* -> disconnectOrderId) without hardcoding in framework.
  • Rule-level field_remaps is still supported for rule-specific overrides when needed.
Latest Contract Changes

SemanticField supports optional aliases in YAML. SemanticModel supports top-level value_patterns (global remaps), and SemanticIntentRule supports optional field_remaps (rule-specific remaps), all mapped via SemanticIntentFieldRemap.

8) settings, join_hints, rules, and other descriptive tags​

Snippet: currently ignored-by-loader tags
YAML

Current status:

  • These tags are excellent documentation for model authors.
  • They are not currently bound to SemanticModel records, so runtime ignores them.
  • Effective runtime limit/timezone/dialect come from application.yml (convengine.mcp.db.semantic.*).
  • DML/DDL guardrails are enforced by SQL policy/guardrail steps, not YAML rules.

Other descriptive tags in sample:

  • example_questions, allowed_values, example are currently prompt/documentation aids and are not strict runtime-enforced model fields.

AST + SQL impact walkthrough​

User question:

  • show billbank gaps with request and order details

LLM generated AST (problem shape):

Invalid mixed AST (detail + metric)
JSON

Why validator rejects:

  • Metrics + detailed select fields requires grouping.
  • With empty group_by, SQL semantics are invalid.

With current normalizer fix:

  • Mixed aggregate/detail AST is demoted to detail query.
  • metrics/having/group_by are removed deterministically before validation.
Normalized AST after fix (detail mode)
JSON

Typical SQL shape after fix:

Compiled SQL shape (detail mode)
SQL

If you want aggregate output intentionally, prompt explicitly:

  • show billbank gap count by accountId

Semantic Query Tool Runtime Deep Dive​

This section explains the runtime tool path that starts at db.semantic.query and executes retrieval -> join path -> AST -> SQL -> summary in deterministic stage order.

Exact source

Runtime files below show how the semantic tool call is orchestrated from handler entry to summarized result.

High-level code execution order (runtime)​

1DbSemanticQueryToolHandler.java receives tool request and calls runtime service.
2Runtime orchestration handoff flows into Result Summary/ Wrapper Deep Dive where wrapper behavior is documented.
3Interceptors: SemanticQueryStageInterceptor.java + DefaultSemanticQueryStageInterceptor.java.
4Pipeline wiring: SemanticStagePipelineFactory.java -> SemanticStagePipeline.java.
5Stage contract/context: SemanticQueryStage.java + SemanticQueryContext.java.
6Execution stages: SemanticRetrievalStage.java -> SemanticJoinPathStage.java -> SemanticAstGenerationStage.java -> SemanticAstValidationStage.java -> SemanticSqlCompileStage.java -> SemanticSqlExecuteStage.java -> SemanticResultSummaryStage.java.

DbSemanticQueryToolHandler.java​

DbSemanticQueryToolHandler.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/runtime/handler/DbSemanticQueryToolHandler.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: MCP tool handler entrypoint for semantic query execution.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Receives db.semantic.query tool args, delegates to runtime service, and returns structured semantic result envelope.
      1. Integrates with stage audit trail and deterministic runtime transitions.

SemanticQueryStageInterceptor.java​

SemanticQueryStageInterceptor.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/runtime/core/SemanticQueryStageInterceptor.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: Runtime stage interceptor contract.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Provides before/after/error hooks around each semantic runtime stage for extension and audit customization.
      1. Integrates with stage audit trail and deterministic runtime transitions.

DefaultSemanticQueryStageInterceptor.java​

DefaultSemanticQueryStageInterceptor.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/runtime/core/DefaultSemanticQueryStageInterceptor.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: Default no-op stage interceptor implementation.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Establishes ordered extension point without changing stage behavior.
      1. Integrates with stage audit trail and deterministic runtime transitions.

SemanticStagePipelineFactory.java​

SemanticStagePipelineFactory.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/runtime/pipeline/SemanticStagePipelineFactory.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: Builds ordered stage list used by semantic runtime.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Resolves stage beans, applies ordering/dependencies, and materializes the immutable stage pipeline instance.
      1. Integrates with stage audit trail and deterministic runtime transitions.

SemanticStagePipeline.java​

SemanticStagePipeline.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/runtime/pipeline/SemanticStagePipeline.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: Executes resolved stage list in sequence.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Invokes each stage with shared context and stops via exception on stage failure.
      1. Integrates with stage audit trail and deterministic runtime transitions.

SemanticQueryContext.java​

SemanticQueryContext.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/runtime/stage/context/SemanticQueryContext.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: Shared mutable context across semantic runtime stages.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Carries question, retrieval, join-path, AST, compiled SQL, execution rows, and summary between stages.
      1. Integrates with stage audit trail and deterministic runtime transitions.

SemanticQueryStage.java​

SemanticQueryStage.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/runtime/stage/core/SemanticQueryStage.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: Stage SPI contract for semantic runtime pipeline.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Each stage exposes stageCode() and execute(context) to support deterministic stage orchestration.
      1. Integrates with stage audit trail and deterministic runtime transitions.

SemanticRetrievalStage.java​

SemanticRetrievalStage.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/runtime/stage/provider/SemanticRetrievalStage.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: Stage that resolves candidate entities/tables from user question.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Invokes retriever, stores RetrievalResult in context, and emits RETRIEVAL_DONE audit metadata.
      1. Integrates with stage audit trail and deterministic runtime transitions.

SemanticJoinPathStage.java​

SemanticJoinPathStage.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/runtime/stage/provider/SemanticJoinPathStage.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: Stage that resolves join traversal plan from retrieval output.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Computes base table + edges + initial required tables and emits JOIN_PATH_RESOLVED audit details.
      1. Final required-table expansion from AST field references is done during SQL compile before query assembly.
      1. Integrates with stage audit trail and deterministic runtime transitions.

SemanticAstGenerationStage.java​

SemanticAstGenerationStage.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/runtime/stage/provider/SemanticAstGenerationStage.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: Stage that generates AST via LLM and canonicalizes it.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Calls AST generator, canonicalizer, emits AST_GENERATED and AST_INTERPRETATION_SUMMARY audit events.
      1. Integrates with stage audit trail and deterministic runtime transitions.

SemanticAstValidationStage.java​

SemanticAstValidationStage.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/runtime/stage/provider/SemanticAstValidationStage.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: Stage that validates canonical AST against semantic model rules.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Runs validator chain, writes AstValidationResult into context, and fails fast on invalid AST.
      1. Integrates with stage audit trail and deterministic runtime transitions.

SemanticSqlCompileStage.java​

SemanticSqlCompileStage.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/runtime/stage/provider/SemanticSqlCompileStage.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: Stage that compiles canonical AST to parameterized SQL.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Invokes SQL compiler + policy validator and emits SQL_COMPILED with _db SQL details.
      1. Integrates with stage audit trail and deterministic runtime transitions.

SemanticSqlExecuteStage.java​

SemanticSqlExecuteStage.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/runtime/stage/provider/SemanticSqlExecuteStage.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: Stage that executes compiled SQL against datasource.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Runs named-parameter query, stores row payload in context, and emits SQL_EXECUTED / SQL_EXECUTE_ERROR.
      1. Integrates with stage audit trail and deterministic runtime transitions.

SemanticResultSummaryStage.java​

SemanticResultSummaryStage.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/runtime/stage/provider/SemanticResultSummaryStage.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: Stage that turns execution rows into final semantic summary text.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Builds markdown/table style response summary and emits RESULT_SUMMARIZED for planner response consumption.
      1. Integrates with stage audit trail and deterministic runtime transitions.

Retrieval Deep Dive​

This section explains the retrieval package execution flow for semantic query and documents each file with source and stage-level behavior.

Exact source

Retrieval runs before join-path and determines which entity/table context is handed to AST generation.

High-level code execution order​

1SemanticEntityTableRetriever.java entry contract is invoked by SemanticRetrievalStage.
2Hooks run via SemanticRetrievalInterceptor.java (before/after/error).
3DefaultSemanticEntityTableRetriever.java computes deterministic lexical/field/id scores.
4Optional vector path via SemanticVectorSearchAdapter.java and PostgresPgVectorSearchAdapter.java.
5Fallback/no-vector behavior via NoopSemanticVectorSearchAdapter.java.
6Rank rows materialize into CandidateEntity.java and CandidateTable.java.
7Final envelope emitted as RetrievalResult.java to join-path stage.

CandidateEntity.java​

CandidateEntity.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/retrieval/core/CandidateEntity.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: Retrieval score row for entity candidates.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Carries final blended score and score components for audit/debug.
      1. Participates in retrieval scoring/audit contract before AST generation.

CandidateTable.java​

CandidateTable.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/retrieval/core/CandidateTable.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: Retrieval score row for table candidates.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Pairs selected entity context with table-level ranking scores.
      1. Participates in retrieval scoring/audit contract before AST generation.

RetrievalResult.java​

RetrievalResult.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/retrieval/core/RetrievalResult.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: Retrieval stage output envelope.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Feeds join-path stage with candidates and confidence.
      1. Participates in retrieval scoring/audit contract before AST generation.

SemanticEntityTableRetriever.java​

SemanticEntityTableRetriever.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/retrieval/core/SemanticEntityTableRetriever.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: Retriever SPI contract.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Stage resolves implementation by supports(session) and calls retrieve().
      1. Participates in retrieval scoring/audit contract before AST generation.

SemanticRetrievalInterceptor.java​

SemanticRetrievalInterceptor.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/retrieval/core/SemanticRetrievalInterceptor.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: Before/after/error hooks around retrieval.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Lets consumers augment ranking behavior without replacing core retriever.
      1. Participates in retrieval scoring/audit contract before AST generation.

SemanticVectorSearchAdapter.java​

SemanticVectorSearchAdapter.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/retrieval/core/SemanticVectorSearchAdapter.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: Vector adapter SPI for entity/table similarity scores.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Default behavior is safe no-op map when vector path is disabled/unavailable.
      1. Participates in retrieval scoring/audit contract before AST generation.

DefaultSemanticRetrievalInterceptor.java​

DefaultSemanticRetrievalInterceptor.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/retrieval/provider/DefaultSemanticRetrievalInterceptor.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: Default pass-through retrieval interceptor bean.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Reserves extension point with deterministic ordering.
      1. Participates in retrieval scoring/audit contract before AST generation.

NoopSemanticVectorSearchAdapter.java​

NoopSemanticVectorSearchAdapter.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/retrieval/provider/NoopSemanticVectorSearchAdapter.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: Fallback vector adapter bean.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Ensures retrieval does not fail when no vector backend is active.
      1. Participates in retrieval scoring/audit contract before AST generation.

PostgresPgVectorSearchAdapter.java​

PostgresPgVectorSearchAdapter.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/retrieval/provider/PostgresPgVectorSearchAdapter.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: pgvector-backed vector similarity implementation.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Returns normalized similarity scores for ENTITY and TABLE target types.
      1. Participates in retrieval scoring/audit contract before AST generation.

DefaultSemanticEntityTableRetriever.java​

DefaultSemanticEntityTableRetriever.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/retrieval/provider/DefaultSemanticEntityTableRetriever.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: Main deterministic+vector retrieval engine.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Tokenizes question, computes lexical/field/id scores, blends vector + feedback boosts, and emits ranked candidates.
      1. Participates in retrieval scoring/audit contract before AST generation.

Join-Path Deep Dive​

This section explains how semantic runtime moves from retrieval candidates to a deterministic join path and planning context for SQL compilation.

Exact source

Join-path logic decides table traversal; SQL compiler then renders joins from this resolved plan.

High-level code execution order (join-path)​

1Graph contracts: SchemaGraphEngine.java, SchemaEdge.java, SchemaGraphInterceptor.java.
2Resolver contracts: JoinPathResolver.java -> JoinPathPlan.java.
3Planner contracts: AstPlanner.java + AstPlanningInterceptor.java.
4Provider implementations: JGraphTSchemaGraphEngine.java + DefaultJoinPathResolver.java.
5Planning providers: DefaultAstPlanner.java + DefaultAstPlanningInterceptor.java + DefaultSchemaGraphInterceptor.java.

SchemaGraphEngine.java​

SchemaGraphEngine.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/graph/core/SchemaGraphEngine.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: Graph engine contract for schema traversal.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Exposes graph traversal API used by join-path resolver to discover table connectivity.
      1. Supplies deterministic join-planning context to AST validation/SQL compile stages.

SchemaGraphInterceptor.java​

SchemaGraphInterceptor.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/graph/core/SchemaGraphInterceptor.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: Graph build/resolve interceptor contract.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Allows before/after/error extension hooks around schema graph operations.
      1. Supplies deterministic join-planning context to AST validation/SQL compile stages.

SchemaEdge.java​

SchemaEdge.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/graph/core/SchemaEdge.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: Join edge model between two schema tables.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Captures source/target tables and join key metadata used by planner/compiler.
      1. Supplies deterministic join-planning context to AST validation/SQL compile stages.

JoinPathResolver.java​

JoinPathResolver.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/graph/core/JoinPathResolver.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: Join path resolver contract.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Takes retrieval candidates and resolves base table plus required edge chain.
      1. Supplies deterministic join-planning context to AST validation/SQL compile stages.

JoinPathPlan.java​

JoinPathPlan.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/graph/core/JoinPathPlan.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: Resolved join-path output envelope.
  • Detailed control flow:
      1. Carries base table, resolved edges, unresolved tables, and confidence for downstream stages.
      1. Supplies deterministic join-planning context to AST validation/SQL compile stages.

AstPlanner.java​

AstPlanner.java source
file: src/main/java/com/github/salilvnair/convengine/engine/mcp/query/semantic/graph/core/AstPlanner.java
JAVA

What this file does:

  • Runtime role: Planner contract to align AST with resolved join-path.
